title: Interpret Tote Fill Thresholds To Understand Exchange Readiness
summary: Use the source-defined tote fill thresholds to understand whether a tote is nearing exchange consideration or is full and unavailable for additional diversion.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing tote or location status in the parcel lifecycle context and you need to interpret the source-defined meanings of the tote fullness states "Pretty full" and "Full."
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a tote exchange procedure.
Do not use this runbook to define system responses, controls, or exchange actions beyond the threshold meanings stated in the source.
Do not use this runbook if the observed tote state cannot be matched to the documented thresholds.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the tote or location status and interpret the documented threshold meaning using the source-provided definitions only.

safety_notes: This is a reference interpretation procedure derived from training content and does not provide physical exchange instructions.
Do not invent exchange actions or system responses beyond the threshold meanings stated in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the documented tote threshold information
Visibility into the tote or location status being reviewed

access_required: Access to the training content or extracted threshold reference
system_preconditions: A tote or location status is being reviewed in the parcel lifecycle context

visual_references.description: Training slide showing parcel flow into a chute and tote, along with the tote fullness thresholds and exchange-related messaging.

steps.title: Identify the tote or location in parcel lifecycle context
steps.purpose: Establish the source-defined context for the threshold interpretation.
steps.instruction: Identify the tote or location being discussed in the parcel lifecycle context, where a parcel sorts down a chute into a tote in a rack.
steps.expected_result: The operator is looking at the correct tote or location context for threshold interpretation.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote or location is understood as the destination where parcels sort down a chute into a tote in a rack.
steps.failure_condition: The tote or location under review is unclear or cannot be tied to the parcel lifecycle context described in the source.

actions.label: Identify tote context
actions.exact_action: Identify the tote or location being discussed in the parcel lifecycle context, where parcels sort down a chute into a tote in a rack.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The slide area showing the parcel sorting down a chute into a tote in a rack.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the tote or location being reviewed cannot be identified in the parcel lifecycle context.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The slide explains that a parcel sorts down to a chute and into a tote in a rack.

steps.title: Check the documented tote fullness state
steps.purpose: Determine which of the source-defined threshold states applies.
steps.instruction: Check the documented tote fullness state using the source-provided thresholds "Pretty full" and "Full."
steps.expected_result: The tote state is matched to one of the documented threshold labels.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed state can be matched to the documented threshold labels "Pretty full" or "Full."
steps.failure_condition: The observed state cannot be matched to the documented thresholds.

actions.label: Review threshold labels
actions.exact_action: Check the documented tote fullness state using the source-provided thresholds "Pretty full" and "Full."

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The slide section showing the two tote fill thresholds: "Pretty full" and "Full."

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed tote state cannot be matched to the documented thresholds.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Two thresholds exist: "Pretty full" — not full, but getting close enough to start considering an exchange; "Full" — no more product can be diverted to this location until an exchange is done.

steps.title: Interpret the meaning of pretty full
steps.purpose: Apply the source definition for the intermediate threshold state.
steps.instruction: Interpret "Pretty full" as not full yet, but close enough to start considering an exchange.
steps.expected_result: The operator understands that "Pretty full" indicates nearing exchange readiness, not a fully blocked location.
steps.healthy_condition: "Pretty full" is understood as not full, but close enough to start considering an exchange.
steps.failure_condition: "Pretty full" is treated as fully unavailable or is interpreted using unsupported meanings.

actions.label: Interpret pretty full
actions.exact_action: Interpret "Pretty full" as not full yet, but close enough to start considering an exchange.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if "Pretty full" cannot be interpreted using the source-provided definition only.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: "Pretty full" — not full, but getting close enough to start considering an exchange.

steps.title: Interpret the meaning of full
steps.purpose: Apply the source definition for the full threshold state.
steps.instruction: Interpret "Full" as no more product can be diverted to this location until an exchange is done.
steps.expected_result: The operator understands that a full tote is unavailable for additional diversion until exchange is completed.
steps.healthy_condition: "Full" is understood as a state where no more product can be diverted to the location until an exchange is done.
steps.failure_condition: "Full" is interpreted as still available for diversion or is interpreted using unsupported actions or controls.

actions.label: Interpret full
actions.exact_action: Interpret "Full" as no more product can be diverted to this location until an exchange is done.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The threshold definition for "Full" and the exchange-related note indicating the location is unavailable for additional diversion.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the full-state meaning cannot be matched to the source definition.
Stop if additional exchange actions or system responses would need to be assumed beyond what the source states.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: "Full" — no more product can be diverted to this location until an exchange is done.
When a tote is full, the system tells Chat that the chute is unavailable so no packages come down during an exchange.

steps.title: Record the threshold meaning using source definitions only
steps.purpose: Document the interpretation without adding unsupported operational detail.
steps.instruction: Record the observed threshold meaning using only the source-provided definitions.
steps.expected_result: The threshold meaning is documented as either nearing exchange consideration or full and unavailable for additional diversion until exchange is done.
steps.healthy_condition: The recorded meaning matches the source wording and does not add unsupported exchange procedure details.
steps.failure_condition: The recorded meaning includes invented exchange actions, system responses, or unsupported thresholds.

actions.label: Document threshold meaning
actions.exact_action: Record the observed threshold meaning using only the source-provided definitions.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed tote state cannot be matched to the documented thresholds.
Stop if recording the result would require inventing exchange actions or system responses beyond the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Two thresholds exist: "Pretty full" — not full, but getting close enough to start considering an exchange; "Full" — no more product can be diverted to this location until an exchange is done.

success_criteria: The user can distinguish between "Pretty full" and "Full" using the source definitions.
The user understands that "Pretty full" means not full yet but close enough to start considering an exchange.
The user understands that "Full" means no more product can be diverted to the location until an exchange is done.
healthy_conditions: The tote or location state can be matched to the documented thresholds.
Threshold meanings are interpreted using only the source-provided definitions.
Recorded interpretation does not add unsupported exchange procedure details.
failure_conditions: The observed tote state cannot be matched to the documented thresholds.
The threshold meaning is interpreted with invented actions or unsupported system behavior.
The user treats this reference as a full tote exchange procedure.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed tote state cannot be matched to the documented thresholds.
Escalate if additional operational action is needed beyond the threshold meanings stated in the source.
Do not invent exchange actions or system responses beyond the threshold meanings stated in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Parcel flow into the tote in the rack and the threshold definitions for "Pretty full" and "Full."

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Two thresholds exist: "Pretty full" — not full, but getting close enough to start considering an exchange; "Full" — no more product can be diverted to this location until an exchange is done.

missing_details: The source explains threshold meanings but does not provide a full tote exchange procedure.
The source does not provide a numeric threshold value for "Pretty full" or "Full" in this packet.
The source does not provide a formal recording method, system screen, or field for documenting the observed threshold meaning.
The source does not define role boundaries beyond operator-level interpretation.
